# PlacementPrototype

## Summary
The `PlacementPrototype` is a card definition that implements an interactive prototype for placing different types of cards (represented as "payloads") into various target containers (represented as "targets"). It allows users to drag-and-drop payloads onto compatible targets, with the ghost representation of the payload morphing to match the target's format. The prototype also includes features like a palette for quickly placing payloads, an audit trail of all placement actions, and support for asynchronous cross-realm placements.

## Import
```javascript
import { CardDef, Component } from 'https://cardstack.com/base/card-api';
```

## Usage as a Field
The `PlacementPrototype` is a `CardDef`, so it cannot be used as a field within a consuming card or field.

## Template Usage
To use the `PlacementPrototype` in a template, you can simply include it as a component:

```hbs
<PlacementPrototype />
```

The prototype will handle all the user interactions and placement logic, providing a fully functional interactive experience.

Example:

```gts
import { CardDef, Component } from 'https://cardstack.com/base/card-api';

export class MyCard extends CardDef {
  static isolated = class Isolated extends Component<typeof MyCard> {
    <template>
      <PlacementPrototype />
    </template>
  };
}
```
